item.title: Iran Warns US Ships Not to Enter Strait of Hormuz as 'Guide Mission' Launches
item.description: Iran's military warned that any foreign armed force approaching the Strait of Hormuz would be attacked, as the US begins a mission to guide stranded ships through the critical waterway. The standoff continues amid fragile ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Australia and Japan Sign Energy Security Pact Amid Middle East Supply Crisis
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements deepening cooperation on energy and critical minerals, with Australia providing up to A$1.3 billion for critical mineral projects. The pact aims to secure supply chains disrupted by the Iran conflict.

item.title: Hezbollah Suffers Heavy Casualties in Lebanon Fighting, Over 2,600 Dead
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry reports more than 2,600 killed since March 2, with around a fifth being women, children and medics, as Hezbollah battles to reverse its fortunes on the Lebanon front.

item.title: Iran Warns US Navy to Stay Clear of Hormuz as Trump Launches Ship Escort Mission
item.description: Iran's military warned US forces not to enter the Strait of Hormuz after President Trump announced 'Project Freedom', a US-led operation to guide stranded ships out of the critical waterway. CENTCOM says 15,000 personnel and over 100 aircraft will support the mission.

item.title: US Begins Escorting Stranded Ships Out of Hormuz Under 'Project Freedom'
item.description: A US-led task force has begun operations to guide merchant ships stranded for months out of the Strait of Hormuz. The mission comes after a cargo ship was hit by projectiles near the waterway, one of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoints.

item.title: NATO Chief: Europeans Have Heard Trump's Message on Defense
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said European leaders have 'gotten the message' from Trump over the Iran war and are stepping up defense commitments, following the US announcement of troop withdrawals from Germany.

item.title: AP Analysis: US Military Presence in Europe Under Spotlight
item.description: President Trump's pledge to shrink America's military deployment in Germany is putting new focus on the US role in Europe, which acts as both a deterrent to Russia and a platform for projecting American power globally.

item.title: European NATO Defense Spending Jumps 20% in 2025 as US Share Declines
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte reported a one-fifth real-terms increase in defense spending by European allies and Canada, with all members meeting the 2% GDP target for the first time. The US share of NATO spending fell from 64% to 59% as European contributions rose to billion.

item.title: Taiwan Deputy FM: Island 'Essential for World's Future' Amid Chinese Pressure
item.description: Taiwan's Deputy Foreign Minister François Wu emphasized the island's strategic importance, noting it produces 95% of advanced semiconductors and 100% of AI chips. The statement comes ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping in mid-May.

item.title: Asia's Economic Model Under Strain as Global Fragmentation Accelerates
item.description: The escalating Strait of Hormuz crisis highlights Asia's vulnerability to geopolitical shocks, with nearly one-fifth of global oil passing through the waterway to Asian markets. South Korea, importing over 90% of its energy from the Middle East, exemplifies the region's exposure to Gulf instability.

item.title: IAEA Reports Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Lab
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency says a drone targeted the external radiation control laboratory at Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, raising nuclear safety concerns amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: US Declares Taiwan a Trusted Partner in Message to Eswatini
item.description: The United States called Taiwan a trusted and capable partner and praised its ties with Eswatini, reinforcing Washington's support for the island democracy amid China tensions.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iran
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in an Iranian prison, her foundation reports, as the human rights activist continues to face health deterioration.

item.title: Japan and Australia Deepen Cooperation on Energy Security and Critical Minerals
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements on energy security, defense, and critical minerals cooperation amid the Iran war threatening global supply chains. Japan relies on Australia for nearly half its LNG, and both nations aim to reduce vulnerability to Middle East disruptions.

item.title: China to Expand AI Judicial System After Shenzhen Courts Show 50% Efficiency Gain
item.description: Shenzhen judges processed 50% more cases in 2025 using an AI-powered judicial system, prompting expansion to dozens more Chinese cities. The system covers 85 judicial procedures using domain-specific large language models.

item.title: US Republicans Criticize German Troop Withdrawal Plan
item.description: Chairs of House and Senate armed services committees warn that withdrawing 5,000 US service personnel from Germany would undermine deterrence against Russia at a critical moment.

item.title: Taiwan President Visits Eswatini After China Cancels Trip
item.description: President Tsai Ing-wen arrived in Taiwan's African ally days after Beijing blocked his travel plans. China denounced the visit as a "stowaway-style escape farce."

item.title: Nigeria Summons South African Envoy Over Attacks on Nationals
item.description: Abuja protested to Pretoria following a wave of anti-immigrant violence targeting Nigerian citizens in South Africa. Some protests have turned violent amid rising xenophobic tensions.

item.title: UK to Join EU 78 Billion Pound Ukraine Loan Package
item.description: Britain is set to enter talks to join the European Union massive loan to Ukraine, deepening European defense ties amid rising US pressure for burden sharing.

item.title: Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Technology Partnership
item.description: President Zelenskiy proposed a drone deal with Finland, offering to share combat technology and expertise developed during the four-year war with Russia.

item.title: Swedish Coast Guard Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
item.description: Swedish authorities seized a tanker in the Baltic Sea believed to be part of Russia shadow fleet, the latest in a string of similar actions targeting sanctions evasion.

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Moscow Victory Day Parade
item.description: Robert Fico will be among only a handful of global leaders at Russia scaled-back Victory Day parade, highlighting Slovakia divergent stance within the EU and NATO.

item.title: ECB Warns Euro Zone Recession Risks from Middle East Conflict
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Stournaras said recession concerns are real and justified if the Middle East conflict continues, with Iran war talks key for ECB monetary policy.

item.title: Jerusalem Post Live: Trump Calls Iran Peace Plan "Unacceptable", US Escorts Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Trump rejected Iran's 14-point peace proposal and announced US military will escort stranded vessels through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday. Israel plans major F-35 and F-15 fleet expansion.

item.title: Europe's Fear: Putin's "Window of Opportunity" to Test NATO Commitment
item.description: European governments worry the next two years present Russia with the ideal moment to probe Western resolve on NATO defense commitments amid US distraction with Iran.
